title: carduus pycnocephalus subsp. intermedius
intro: Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. intermedius, also known as intermediate cardoon, is a subspecies of the thistle plant native to southern Europe and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its silymarin and flavonoid content. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as protecting against liver damage, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. intermedius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the plant's leaves and flowers.
